title: INFINITY Project surveys on CH collections practices
content: Do you manage or reuse cultural heritage collections in your work? Then take one of our INFINITY project surveys to share your perspectives and contribute to the future of the digital creative economy. It will take 10 – 15 minutes to complete and the deadline for submissions is 30 April 2026.
snippet: Do you manage or reuse cultural heritage collections in your work? Then take one of our INFINITY project surveys to share your perspectives and contribute to the future of the digital creative economy
creator: Sheena_B
sourceDescription: European Cloud for Heritage OpEn Science

title: INP Focus Group for curators, art historians and conservator-restorers
content: At the end of March the INP hosted a focus group organised by the ECHOES project, bringing together curators, art historians, and conservator-restorers. The event gathered heritage professionals and researchers from across Europe.

title: ECHOLOT Kick-off and Consortium Meetings
content: Although the ECHOLOT project has only been operational for a few months, it has already held two plenary meetings, demonstrating strong engagement and enthusiasm for shaping the future of cultural heritage data in Europe.

title: Round up from ECHOES in Poznań, Poland
content:
The 2nd Annual ECHOES meeting in Poznań buzzed with activity and excitement for the week 16th-20th March with a packed agenda for project members. Days 1-2 consisted of ECHOES internal meetings to present and discuss the project progress and results and to plan for the upcoming months. On Day 3, the morning involved the sister projects and the afternoon opened up ECHOES to all stakeholders at a Public Event. Day 4 involved the Advisory Boards and Sister projects with Day 5 rounding up the week with internal project meetings.

title: Take part in the PlaceMus XR European study on musical heritage and XR
content: The European Horizon PlaceMUS XR project is currently conducting a study on the promotion and reception of European musical heritage through the design of visitor itineraries integrating immersive technologies.

title: MusicSphere featured in a local TV news programme
content: MusicSphere has recorded the sound of an ancient organ in Ilmenau, Germany, as part of their research and this was recently featured on a local news programme, Thüringen Journal by Mitteldeutscher Rundfunk (MDR).

title: Joint Statement from the Europeana Initiative and the ECHOES project on the complementarity between the common European data space for cultural heritage and the European Collaborative Cloud for Cultural Heritage
content:
ECHOES and Europeana met in Poznań on 18 March 2026 to sign the Joint Statement from the Europeana Initiative and the ECHOES project which formalises the cooperation between the Cultural Heritage Cloud and the common European data space for cultural heritage.

title: ECHOES at the HeriTech Forum, Tours, 6th-7th May
content:
The city of Tours, France, will host the 5th edition of the HeriTech Forum, the national event for heritage innovation, in partnership with the Centre-Val de Loire Region on May 6th and 7th. More than 400 stakeholders in heritage, culture, education, and tourism will come to discover and experiment with new approaches and perspectives on territorial storytelling through innovation. Two days to imagine roadmaps and uses of heritage innovation at the territorial level, as a lever for experience, attractiveness, and cultural and tourism innovation.

For its 5th edition, the HeriTech Forum is offering a new format:</

title: ECHOES at the HERITALISE Clustering Event
content: On Friday the 6th March, HERITALISE hosted a Clustering event which was held in the beautiful Castello del Valentino. The programme featured speakers from the Politecnico di Torino (POLITO), HERITALISE, CNR-ISTI, Archivio Restauri del Centro Conservazione e Restauro Venaria (CCRV), EMA and PRISMA Cultura.

title: Joint Statement from the Europeana Initiative and the ECHOES project
content:
on the complementarity between the common European data space for cultural heritage and the European Collaborative Cloud for Cultural Heritage

Poznań, 18 March 2026

The common European data space for cultural heritage (‘data space’) is Europe’s public infrastructure to democratise access to high-quality, multilingual cultural heritage data. It makes digital cultural heritage assets and their metadata – source data – easy to share, find, access and reuse. It supports a wide range of professional and personal uses, from education and research to creative industries, tourism, media, and AI devel
